problem:
A frequency distribution of the scores for Mr. Sampson's algebra class is shown. What percent of the class received a score in the $60\%$-$69\%$ range? \begin{tabular}{|c|c|}
Test Scores & Frequencies\\
\hline
$90\% - 100\%$& IIII\\
$80\% - 89\%$& IIII IIII\\
$70\% - 79\%$& IIII II\\
$60\% - 69\%$ & IIII I\\
Below $60\%$ & II
\end{tabular}

classified_questions.question: What is the frequency distribution showing in the table?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How many students scored in the $60\%-69\%$ range?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How many students are in Mr. Sampson's algebra class in total?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Why do we need to count tallies to find the number of students in each score range?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How do we calculate the percentage of students who received a score in the $60\%-69\%$ range?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can we use the frequency distribution table to find the median and mode of the scores? How?
classified_questions.label: b
